user_message: Help me prep the QBR preparation for Nod Publishers. Manager is asking for an update today.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 12:47 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Nod Publishers QBR preparation

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with QBR preparation. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Samantha Singh <samantha.singh@nodpublisher.com>
Date: 2024-01-04 09:30 EST
Subject: Re: QBR preparation Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the QBR preparation proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-03-28
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft), Michelle (Nod Publishers)

[00:02:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:06:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on security compliance and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:10:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

[00:15:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:20:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:22:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:25:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:28:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:33:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-F trap (Omits likely objections section); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-F failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-F failure mode (Omits likely objections section)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-F -- Omits likely objections section
